channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Former Antique Governor and Officials Face Graft Charges Over P531 Million Government Center Project
item.description: Former Antique governor Rhodora Cadiao, together with eight other provincial officials, has been charged with graft in connection to the P531.2 million construction of the Antique Government Center. T...

item.title: Minister Urges Tanzanian Universities to Develop Local Textbooks for Relevant Education
item.description: The Tanzanian Government, through the Minister for Education, has urged universities to start producing their own textbooks instead of depending on foreign ones mainly from the US and Europe. This cal...

item.title: PM Modi Assures Energy Stability Amid LPG Supply Concerns
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, addressing the NXT Summit 2026 in Delhi, strongly criticised opposition parties who are creating panic about LPG shortages, stating that they are 'exposing themselves.' H...

item.title: Universities urged to champion gender equality through policies and support for women
item.description: Universities in Tanzania and beyond are being called upon to go further than just teaching and doing research; they must take the lead in promoting gender equality and influencing the whole society. T...

item.title: Israeli Military Withdraws Charges Against Five Soldiers in Sde Teiman Abuse Case
item.description: In a controversial move that has drawn global attention, the Israeli military announced on Thursday that it has dropped all charges against five soldiers previously accused of sexually assaulting a Pa...

item.title: India Reviews Fuel Supply Requests from Neighbouring Countries Amid Energy Crisis
item.description: India is currently reviewing requests for fuel supply from Bangladesh, Maldives, and Sri Lanka in light of the ongoing energy crisis triggered by the West Asia conflict. The External Affairs Ministry ...

item.title: Kirinyaga JSS Teacher Found Dead in River, Boyfriend Suspected
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Kirinyaga County where Beaty Wanjiru Nyaga, a 28-year-old Junior Secondary School teacher at Ithiga ria Njuki Primary School, was found dead in River Nyamindi. She had go...

item.title: Weekly South Africanised Cryptic Crossword 521 by George Euvrard
item.description: The Mail & Guardian presents its weekly cryptic crossword, JDE 521, compiled by George Euvrard. This crossword is uniquely South African, featuring a blend of multilingual elements including Afrikaans...

item.title: Government starts construction of 35 neonatal wards to improve newborn care nationwide
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The Tanzanian government has officially started constructing 35 new neonatal wards across the country, with 15 set for regional hospitals and 20 in council hospitals. Deputy Minister f...

item.title: Cameron Smith raises concerns over Adam Reynolds' fitness for upcoming Broncos games
item.description: Cameron Smith has highlighted the tough decision that Brisbane Broncos coach Michael Maguire now faces following an injury to star halfback Adam Reynolds. Reynolds, a key playmaker for the Broncos, ha...

item.title: Ex-ASIO Chief Resigns from Bondi Terror Attack Inquiry, Raising Concerns Among Families
item.description: Grieving families of victims of the Bondi terror attack have expressed deep concern following the sudden resignation of Dennis Richardson, a former ASIO chief and US ambassador, from the royal commiss...

item.title: Windsor-Essex sees early wildlife activity with first turtle rescue of the season
item.description: As temperatures fluctuate in Windsor-Essex, local wildlife is beginning to emerge from winter dormancy. A recent early rescue of a turtle highlighted the start of the region's seasonal wildlife activi...

item.title: Iranian Hacker Group Handala Linked to Retaliatory Cyberattacks on US and Israeli Targets
item.description: Since late February, following a series of air strikes by the United States and Israel on Iran, cybersecurity experts have warned of potential Iranian retaliatory attacks. These warnings became a real...

item.title: Suspended Police Official’s Alleged Loan and Luxury Purchases Under Scrutiny at Commission
item.description: Suspended police organised crime boss Major-General Richard Shibiri appeared before the Madlanga commission following allegations of receiving a R70,000 loan from businessman Vusimusi 'Cat' Matlala. S...

item.title: Tinubu boosts military support as security forces revise tactics against insurgents
item.description: Na today, March 12, 2026, wey President Bola Ahmed Tinubu don approve more military equipment to support Nigeria security forces for fight against insurgents and bandits nationwide. Defence Minister, ...

item.title: Authorities respond to shooting and vehicle ramming at Temple Israel in West Bloomfield, Michigan
item.description: On Thursday afternoon, an active shooter incident occurred at Temple Israel, a synagogue in West Bloomfield, Michigan. The incident reportedly began shortly before 1 p.m., with reports of smoke emergi...

item.title: Controller of Budget Flags Unbudgeted Sh44.5m Spend by Deputy President's Spouse Office
item.description: Controller of Budget Margaret Nyakang’o has expressed regret over an accounting error where the Office of the Spouse of Deputy President Kithure Kindiki spent Sh44.52 million in the first half of the ...

item.title: Authorities seize P204 million shabu in Occidental Mindoro waters
item.description: Authorities in Occidental Mindoro discovered another batch of illegal drugs as 30 kilos of shabu, estimated at P204 million, were found floating near Paluan. The discovery was close to the site where ...

item.title: Archie Moore Explores His Father’s Life Through Gold at the Adelaide Biennial
item.description: Archie Moore’s latest exhibition, Remnants Of My Father (2025), is part of the 2026 Adelaide Biennial of Australian Art, curated by Ellie Buttrose. The display at Adelaide’s Museum of Economic Botany ...

item.title: Middle East conflict escalates as Iran and Israel intensify military actions; millions displaced
